title: Used electronics imports remain poorly counted
body: Customs data does not cleanly separate new electronics from used equipment, so the true import burden remains uncertain.

title: Domestic generation and import burdens are still contested
body: The 500,000-tonne domestic figure and the 1.1 million-tonne total burden estimate do not rest on a single harmonised methodology.

title: Child labour in Nigeria's e-waste sector remains under-researched
body: Concern is justified, but the evidence base remains much thinner than it should be for direct measurement and targeted policy design.

title: Health studies cluster around the best-known sites
body: The strongest evidence comes from major dumping and processing zones, leaving smaller or less visible sites comparatively undocumented.

title: Nigeria-specific growth projections are still weak
body: Global e-waste growth projections offer a warning, but the country lacks a strong Nigeria-specific forecast that matches its faster digital adoption curve.
